commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Hearing. Though detraction may assail him, he shall not fear, (Calmet) since God is the judge. (Haydock)
He shall have no cause to apprehend being condemned, (St. Jerome) nor be disturbed about "news "because his goods are in a place of safety, (St. Chrysostom) where thieves cannot steal. (Haydock)
